summary.intent: Fix review comments on PR #497 in the moltis repo, addressing a missing comment and an ordering inconsistency flagged by the greptile bot.
summary.outcome: Both issues were resolved: added the missing `fetch_models` comment in the Fireworks template block and corrected the ordering of Fireworks in `OPENAI_COMPAT_PROVIDERS` (moved to between `deepseek` and `mistral`). Changes were committed and pushed.

repo: OPENAI_COMPAT_PROVIDERS in crates/providers/src/lib.rs maintains alphabetical/conventional ordering: deepseek, fireworks, mistral (and venice before deepseek).
Fireworks provider has a template block in crates/config/src/template.rs that requires a `fetch_models` comment consistent with other providers.

code.finding: OPENAI_COMPAT_PROVIDERS list order matters; Fireworks belongs after deepseek and before mistral.
Fireworks template block was missing a `fetch_models` comment that other provider blocks have.

workflow: Greptile bot performs automated review on PRs and flags ordering and documentation inconsistencies.
Tests were run after edits to verify nothing broke before committing.

friction: Greptile bot comments required manual cross-referencing of provider ordering across two files.
